---
title: "Phase 2 — HLWAS expansion per skycell"
subtitle: "For each selected skycell, enumerate every HLWAS F158 (pointing, SCA) that overlaps"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np, pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from astropy.table import Table
REPO = Path("../..").resolve()
SKY = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/selected_skycells.ecsv",
                 format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
NEED_SIM = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/pointings_sca_to_simulate.ecsv",
                      format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
```

## What Phase 2 does

Phase 2 is purely geometric — no image data is simulated. For each of
the 12 skycells selected in [Phase 1](phase1_pointings.qmd):

1. Gather all HLWAS F158 (Wide-Field2) exposures whose nominal centre
   is within 0.6° of the skycell centre — the pool of candidate
   pointings whose outer SCAs could plausibly reach the 4.6′ skycell.
2. For each (candidate pointing, SCA) pair, build the CRDS-backed WCS
   via `romanisim.wcs.get_wcs(meta_dict, usecrds=True)` with
   `fill_in_parameters(coord, pa_aper, boresight=False)` — the same
   code path `romanisim-make-image --usecrds` uses.
3. Take the 4 SCA corners and call
   `romancal.skycell.match.find_skycell_matches(corners)`. If the
   selected skycell is in the match list, add the (pointing, SCA)
   to Phase 4's sim list.

All L2s per skycell are simulated at the skycell's SIM_DATE (from
Phase 1, chosen for ~90° solar elongation). That fixes zodi across
the mosaic regardless of what HLWAS PASS the original visit was
scheduled in.

## L2 counts per skycell

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "L2 files per pair after HLWAS expansion. `n_l2_files` is the distinct (PASS × SEGMENT × VISIT × EXPOSURE × SCA) combos whose SCA overlaps the target skycell; `n_visits` is the number of distinct HLWAS visits contributing. Pairs 9 and 10 got 0 overlap — HLWAS tiling near the ecliptic leaves coverage gaps."
dedup_key = ["PASS", "SEGMENT", "OBSERVATION", "VISIT", "EXPOSURE", "SCA"]
visits_key = ["PASS", "SEGMENT", "OBSERVATION", "VISIT"]

l2_counts = (NEED_SIM.drop_duplicates(["PAIR_ID"] + dedup_key)
             .groupby("PAIR_ID").size().reset_index(name="n_l2_files"))
l2_counts["n_visits"] = (NEED_SIM.drop_duplicates(["PAIR_ID"] + visits_key)
                         .groupby("PAIR_ID").size().values)
summary = SKY[["PAIR_ID", "skycell_name", "zodi_bin", "coverage_bin",
                "count_0p25"]].merge(l2_counts, on="PAIR_ID", how="left")
summary["n_l2_files"] = summary["n_l2_files"].fillna(0).astype(int)
summary["n_visits"] = summary["n_visits"].fillna(0).astype(int)
summary
```

## Per-skycell coverage maps

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Per-skycell SCA footprint overlays. Red square = the 4.6′ skycell; each coloured polygon = one contributing L2's SCA footprint. Plots show, visually, how coverage density varies with pair_id."
from IPython.display import Image, display
from pathlib import Path as P
for pid in sorted(NEED_SIM["PAIR_ID"].unique()):
    path = REPO / f"output/detection/phase2/coverage_pair_{pid}.png"
    if P(str(path)).exists():
        display(Image(filename=str(path)))
```

## Design consequence — variable per-pixel depth across the sample

Low-coverage skycells (pairs 1, 2, 5, 6) have only ~2 L2 files each
after expansion — outer SCAs of ~1-2 nearby HLWAS visits. Per-pixel
depth will be 1-2 in most of those mosaics.

High-coverage skycells (pairs 3, 4, 7, 8, 11, 12) have 16-26 L2
files each, with per-pixel depth ~8-12 expected based on pair 11119's
earlier validation.

That dynamic range — factor of 10× in depth across the sample — is the
point of the matrix design: Phase 5 measures detection efficiency vs
position, source type, magnitude, *and* depth simultaneously.

## Reproducing

```bash
pixi run python scripts/detection/02_skycell_overlap.py
```

Reads `selected_skycells.ecsv` (Phase 1), writes
`pointings_sca_to_simulate.ecsv` (the Phase 4 input) plus per-pair
coverage plots. Wall time ~85 min at 4-way parallelism (one
CRDS-backed get_wcs × find_skycell_matches call takes a few seconds;
the expansion loops over `# candidates × 18 SCAs` per pair).

## Checkpoint

Phase 2 done — 10 of 12 skycells have non-zero L2 coverage. The two
skipped (pairs 9, 10 in the `high_zodi × low_coverage` cell) are
documented in Phase 1.
